summary: svgson-lite ships hidden getPlugin() that eval's remote JSON payload
details: index.js exports an undocumented function getPlugin() whose returned callback issues an HTTP request to https://jsonkeeper.com/b/DROGR and passes the response JSON's `model` field to eval(). Any consumer that invokes this export executes attacker-controlled JavaScript in the installer's Node process, giving the publisher of the jsonkeeper paste arbitrary remote code execution on the host. The package.json describes svgson-lite as a 'Tiny zero-dependency SVG helper', which contradicts the actual code (requires the `request` module and exposes network+eval surface) — a cover-story shape typical of a trojaned helper. jsonkeeper.com is a public, mutable paste host, so the executed code can be changed by the publisher at any time without republishing the package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
